Inspect or update the loaded Laurel config

Usage:
  laurel config [--config <path>] [--json] [--format <json|toml>] <subcommand...>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `print` (dump the fully resolved config after defaults, env overrides, and config layers), `validate` (load config only and exit 0/1), `get <dotted.key>` (print one value), `set <dotted.key> <value>` (write a string/number/bool), or `path` (print the detected config path and project .laurelrc path/status)

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `laurel new post -- --config`.

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -j, --json          Emit the value as JSON. For `print`: equivalent to `--format json`. For `validate`: emit `{ ok, errors }`. For `get`: pretty-printed JSON of the value at the dotted path. For `set`: a `{ "config_path": "..." }` envelope. For `path`: a `{ "config_path": "...", "rc_path": "..." }` envelope so CI consumers can branch on `null` for missing files.
  --format <json|toml>  For `print`, choose the resolved config output format: `toml` (default) or `json`.
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → LAUREL_CONFIG_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel config print                          # resolved config as TOML
  laurel config print --format json            # resolved config as JSON
  laurel config validate                       # config-only validation
  laurel config path                           # detected config and .laurelrc paths
  laurel config get site.url
  laurel config set site.title "My Site"
  laurel config set components.rss.enabled false
  laurel config get build.base_path --json
